text:
Quietly, keep your head upright. You calmly notice the sensations in your back as it relaxes and releases, and then become aware of your back. Now that you have become aware of each contact point of your body, you can gently release your head. With your back straight, let your whole head relax and soften; as you notice this, use your eyes to observe your head.

You can gently part your mouth and place your tongue on the floor of your mouth, behind your incisors; now you can gently relax your jaw. On the left, as you become aware of it and let it release, you can calmly come to center yourself in that area. Between your two eyes, your whole head is well relaxed and at ease.

I now invite you to become aware of your neck and shoulders. You will move down very slowly: to your shoulders, along the outer edges of your arms, notice your forearms, then your hands down to the tips of your fingers. And to better become aware of these areas, you will come to gently center yourself at the level...
